problem_statement: After an overnight host replacement, handheld baggage scanners at one airport terminal discarded scans during brief wireless gaps instead of buffering them for reconnection, forcing manual reconciliation at three gates.

observations.statement: The first discarded scan occurred at 04:32 on Terminal C's newly replaced relay hosts; Terminals A and B, still on their prior hosts, buffered the same model of scanner through comparable wireless gaps.
observations.provenance: Airport operations timeline built from relay receipts and independent wireless-controller events.

observations.statement: The effective-settings snapshot from a new Terminal C relay records OFFLINE_GRACE_SECONDS as 0; the approved terminal profile and the prior relay snapshot both record 180.
observations.provenance: Signed startup snapshot compared with the approved deployment record and retained prior-host snapshot.

observations.statement: Provisioning provenance shows a generic zero-value environment entry applied after the Terminal C profile during host bootstrap, so the later entry became effective before the relay first started.
observations.provenance: Immutable bootstrap event stream and rendered launch manifest retained by platform operations.

observations.statement: Application artifact hashes, scanner firmware versions, queue schema, and wireless security policies match across all three terminals.
observations.provenance: Release attestation, device inventory, and wireless policy export from separate systems.

observations.statement: On an isolated relay, changing only OFFLINE_GRACE_SECONDS from 180 to 0 converted a 90-second disconnection from 27 buffered scans to 27 discarded scans; restoring 180 restored buffering.
observations.provenance: Three-phase replay using synthetic bag identifiers and a disabled airline uplink.

observations.statement: Packet captures show scanners retransmitted after each gap as designed, but the new relays had already returned terminal discard acknowledgements; no queue-write attempt appears when the effective value is zero.
observations.provenance: Synchronized scanner-side and relay-side captures retained from a supervised gate drill.

observations.statement: A canary relay launched with the approved 180-second value buffered and forwarded 200 synthetic scans across five induced wireless gaps without contacting production airline systems.
observations.provenance: Safety-reviewed terminal canary exercise witnessed by baggage operations.

observations.statement: After the generic entry was removed and all relays reported 180 at startup, a monitored 36-hour period included 14 wireless gaps and 8,906 scans with no offline discard.
observations.provenance: Post-change relay report reconciled against gate reconciliation logs.
